content.text:
```mdl
@ Contract: OISAIR PROJECT – Two‑Way Confidentiality and Non‑Disclosure Agreement
module contract_nli_no_align_dev_3_nda_17

# -------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Types
# -------------------------------------------------------------------------

@ Parties (Section 1)
type Party = {
    name: string,                # legal name of the party
    is_disclosing: bool,         # true for the Disclosing Party
    is_receiving: bool           # true for the Receiving Party
}

@ Confidential information (Section 1, “Confidential Information” definition)
type ConfidentialInfo = {
    is_marked_confidential: bool,   # true when the information is marked as confidential
    is_oral: bool,                  # true when disclosed orally/visually
    disclosed_by: Party,            # the Disclosing Party
    received_by: Party              # the Receiving Party
}

@ Purpose of the disclosure (Section 1, “Permitted Purpose”)
type Purpose = {
    permitted: bool,    # true when the use is for the permitted purpose
    regulatory: bool    # true when the copy is retained for regulatory/insurance reasons
}

# -------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Entities – stable public objects that can be aligned across modules
# -------------------------------------------------------------------------

@ The two contracting parties
entity disclosing_party: Party
entity receiving_party: Party

@ The confidential material that is exchanged
entity confidential_info: ConfidentialInfo

@ The purpose for which the confidential information may be used
entity purpose: Purpose

@ State variables needed for the copying rules
entity copy_made: bool               # true when a copy of the confidential information exists
entity prior_authorization: bool     # true when the Disclosing Party gave written consent
entity retained_copy: bool           # true when a single copy is retained for regulatory/insurance purposes

# -------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Rules – deontic constraints (obligations O, prohibitions F, permissions P)
# -------------------------------------------------------------------------

# 3a – Reasonable security measures (obligation, always)
@ Clause 3a
rule O take_reasonable_measures:
    true always

# 3b – No disclosure to third parties without written consent (prohibition)
@ Clause 3b
rule F no_third_party_disclosure:
    not (copy_made and not prior_authorization) always

# 3c – No use outside the permitted purpose (prohibition)
@ Clause 3c
rule F use_outside_permitted_purpose:
    not (confidential_info.received_by.is_receiving and not purpose.permitted) always

# 3d – No commercial use without consent (prohibition)
@ Clause 3d
rule F commercial_use_without_consent:
    not (confidential_info.received_by.is_receiving and not prior_authorization) always

# 3e – No copying without prior written authorisation (prohibition)
@ Clause 3e
rule F copy_without_authorisation:
    not (copy_made and not prior_authorization) always

# 3f – No alteration, reverse‑engineering, etc. (prohibition)
@ Clause 3f
rule F alteration_prohibited:
    true always   # (placeholder – actual alteration predicates would be added here)

# 3g – No patent/trademark filing that includes confidential information (prohibition)
@ Clause 3g
rule F patent_filing_prohibited:
    true always   # (placeholder – actual filing predicates would be added here)

# -------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Permissions / Exceptions concerning copying
# -------------------------------------------------------------------------

# The Receiving Party may retain ONE copy for regulatory/insurance purposes
# (Clause 5, “retain one (1) copy … for regulatory or insurance purposes”)
@ Clause 5
rule P regulatory_copy_permission:
    retained_copy implies (purpose.regulatory and prior_authorization) always

# If a copy exists, it must be either authorised or kept for regulatory reasons
@ Clause 5 (implicit)
rule P copy_allowed_if_authorised_or_regulatory:
    copy_made implies (prior_authorization or purpose.regulatory) always

# The retained copy is, by definition, a copy
@ Clause 5 (implicit)
rule O retained_copy_is_a_copy:
    retained_copy implies copy_made always

# The retained copy is, by definition, authorised
@ Clause 5 (implicit)
rule O retained_copy_is_authorised:
    retained_copy implies prior_authorization always

# -------------------------------------------------------------------------
# End of model
# -------------------------------------------------------------------------
```
